failure_modes.text: Early progress can create false mastery when leaders scale responsibility before the capability has actually consolidated.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when early progress is mistaken for mastery and the organization scales responsibility faster than capability has actually consolidated.
failure_modes.mitigation: Verify capability consolidation before scaling responsibility; early progress can create false mastery that collapses under load.

failure_modes.text: Passive exposure can look like learning even when the team has not built the retrieval strength needed for reliable performance.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test for retrieval strength, not just exposure: passive familiarity does not prove the team can perform reliably under pressure.

failure_modes.text: Pattern success can harden into entrenchment when an old framework gets reused mechanically in a new context.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge pattern reuse in new contexts: old frameworks applied mechanically can harden into entrenchment instead of accelerating learning.

premortem_questions.text: Are we judging this capability by smooth early progress or by evidence that it will perform reliably after repetition and feedback?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when performance improves through repetition, feedback, and chunked skill-building rather than one-off inspiration.

premortem_questions.text: If I had to explain the core skill in plain English from memory, where would my explanation fall apart?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If I were to test myself on the main ideas, how exactly would I describe them?

premortem_questions.text: What assumption is embedded in the framework we are reusing, and are those assumptions actually met here?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What assumption is embedded in the framework I am currently using, and are those assumptions met?

heuristics.text: Treat capability as something that compounds through repetition, feedback, and chunked skill-building instead of as a one-shot test.
heuristics.source_quote: Most useful when performance improves through repetition, feedback, and chunked skill-building rather than one-off inspiration.

heuristics.text: Use active recall and self-testing so fluency is measured by retrieval strength rather than by passive familiarity.
Stress-test edge cases and worst-case scenarios so ramp confidence does not rest only on the comfortable base case.
heuristics.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

curation_notes.summary: Focused on compounding skill acquisition, anti-false-fluency, and anti-entrenchment rather than on broad mastery rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor adaptive-strategy sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on how capability consolidates over time.
open_questions: The source reaches into communication and strategy, but the intervention layer stays on the learning ramp and its distortions.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on deep-work, checklists, and analogical reasoning.
antagonists: The source is strongest against entrenchment, availability-style hammering, and unearned fluency.
structured_tensions: The model has meaningful tension around early success being treated as proof of reliable mastery.
